# SL-LINK-014: important-page-excessive-crawl-depth

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

Important indexable pages are deeper than configured crawl-depth guidance.

## Metadata

- Category: Links and site graph (links-site-graph)
- Default severity: info
- Confidence: heuristic
- Scope: site
- Provider scope: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- crawler

## Required Evidence

- page-url
- crawl-depth
- policy-threshold

## When This Triggers

Compute shortest internal path depth and compare with route importance policy.

## False Positives

This rule is heuristic. Treat the diagnostic as a review prompt and use DSL
severity overrides or suppressions when project policy intentionally differs.

## How To Fix

Improve internal linking to important pages.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/links/excessive-crawl-depth

Fixed direction:

- Improve internal linking to important pages.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

## Implementation Surfaces

- Crawler snapshots can provide crawl, robots.txt, sitemap, and site-graph
  evidence.
